text.div:
Expansion performed internally based on codesystem BC Client Registry Address Validation Status Code v1.0.0 (CodeSystem)

This value set contains 3 concepts

SystemCodeDisplay (en)DefinitionJSONXML
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-address-validation-code-system  validvalidThe address is valid, i.e. there is a reasonable good chance this address is known to Canada Post.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-address-validation-code-system  invalidinvalidThe address validation status is invalid and like not recognized by Canada Post.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-address-validation-code-system  unknownunknownThe address validation status is unknown.

Expansion performed internally based on codesystem BC Client Registry Patient Change Notification Events Code System v1.0.0 (CodeSystem)

This value set contains 9 concepts

SystemCodeDisplay (en)DefinitionJSONXML
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-patient-change-notification-events  COMPComposite ViewThe Composite View distribution serves different business purposes depending on whether or not the subscriber is a source system. For a source system, Composite View distributions inform a subscribing system that its records are out-of-sync with the composite view. For a non-source system, Composite View distributions inform a subscriber when a data attribute has been modified on a specific source.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-patient-change-notification-events  DEATHDeathA Death Distributions notifications inform subscribers of both confirmed and unconfirmed deaths. This occurs when just the Date of Death is present, or both the Date of Death and Deceased indicator are present in the Composite View. There are some behavior differences depending on whether or not the subscriber is a source system. For a source system, Death distributions inform a subscriber that they have a record that is out-of-sync with the Date of Death, or the Deceased indicator is added to the Composite View. For a non-source system, Death distributions inform a subscribing system that there is a Date of Death, or the Deceased indicator is added.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-patient-change-notification-events  NEWNewThe NEW distribution is a non-source distribution that informs subscribers of all new records. Any source can trigger a NEW distribution, and subscribers can limit the sources they subscribe to. For instnce, if a subscriber wants to be notified of new PHNs, they subscribe to the MOH_CRS source.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-patient-change-notification-events  NEWBORNNewbornA Newborn distribution is a non-source distribution that informs subscribers that a newborn record has been created and has been assigned a PHN. Typically, subscribers would limit their source subscription to MOH_CRS for this distribution.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-patient-change-notification-events  NEWBORNGENDERNewborn GenderThe Newborn Gender distribution is a non-source distribution and informs subscribers when the gender on a newborn record changes from unknown or undifferentiated to Male or Female. This distribution is used to ensure that pre-registered newborns (where the gender is frequently unknown) are registered for MSP benefits. MSP does not allow the registration of newborns with an unknown gender value.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-patient-change-notification-events  PHNPHNPHN distributions only work for subscribers that are source systems. This distribution informs source systems that their purported PHN is out-of-sync with the PHN in the Composite View. The purported PHN is the PHN sent on the last update by the source system, while the PHN in the Composite View is the MOH_CRS source PHN.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-patient-change-notification-events  PHNJOINPHN JoinA PHNJOIN distribution works for subscribers that are source and non-source systems and informs them that their record has joined an entity with a PHN, or a PHN has joined its entity. For source systems, PHNJOIN distribution informs a subscriber that one of the following has occured: their record has created a PHN, their record has joined the set of an existing PHN, their record moved from a non-survivor PHN to a survivor PHN when the PHNs were merged. For non-source systems, PHNJOIN distribution informs non-source subscribers that a specific source they are listening to has joined a set with a PHN.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-patient-change-notification-events  MERGEMergeMERGE is a non-source distribution and informs subscribers of merged PHNs or Source Record Identifiers (SRIs). Merge distributions can be triggered by any source. All distribution profiles can limit the sources they subscribe to; therefore, if a subscriber only wants to be notified of a PHN merge, they subscribe to the MOH_CRS source. If they want to be notified of a SRI merge, they subscribe to the required source.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-patient-change-notification-events  SSRISYNCSSRI SyncAn SSRI Sync distribution applies only to subscribers that are source systems and are used to notify a parent source system when one of its child systems' records has joined or changed within an entity set. SSRI Sync distributions support PHSA and the Clinical and Systems Transformation project (CST). In this project, multiple sources (VCHA, PHC, and PHSA) are implementing a single Cerner CIS (VPP Cerner) across all their facilities and replacing multiple existing systems. As part of this effort, all patients are issued a new SRI; existing SRIs are recorded and stored for historical use only. The new Cerner CIS is loading the SRI from the old system as the SSRI in the new system. The new CIS will be deployed to facilities over several years, and the patients will be migrated from their existing systems to the new CIS as their facility goes live and the legacy system is decommissioned.

text.div:
Expansion performed internally based on:

  • codesystem ContactPointSystem v4.0.1 (CodeSystem)
  • valueset ContactPointSystem v4.0.1 (ValueSet)

This value set contains 2 concepts

SystemCodeDisplay (en)DefinitionJSONXML
http://hl7.org/fhir/contact-point-system  phonePhoneThe value is a telephone number used for voice calls. Use of full international numbers starting with + is recommended to enable automatic dialing support but not required.
http://hl7.org/fhir/contact-point-system  emailEmailThe value is an email address.

resource.name: ContactPointSystemVS
resource.title: BC Contact Point System Value Set

text.div:
Expansion performed internally based on:

  • codesystem ContactPointUse v4.0.1 (CodeSystem)
  • valueset ContactPointUse v4.0.1 (ValueSet)

This value set contains 3 concepts

SystemCodeDisplay (en)DefinitionJSONXML
http://hl7.org/fhir/contact-point-use  homeHomeA communication contact point at a home; attempted contacts for business purposes might intrude privacy and chances are one will contact family or other household members instead of the person one wishes to call. Typically used with urgent cases, or if no other contacts are available.
http://hl7.org/fhir/contact-point-use  workWorkAn office contact point. First choice for business related contacts during business hours.
http://hl7.org/fhir/contact-point-use  mobileMobileA telecommunication device that moves and stays with its owner. May have characteristics of all other use codes, suitable for urgent matters, not the first choice for routine business.

Expansion performed internally based on codesystem BC Client registry identifier status code system v1.0.0 (CodeSystem)

This value set contains 2 concepts

SystemCodeDisplay (en)DefinitionJSONXML
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-identifier-status-code-system  mergedmergedThe identifier has been merged.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-identifier-status-code-system  activeactiveThe identifier is active.

Expansion performed internally based on:

  • codesystem IdentifierUse v4.0.1 (CodeSystem)
  • valueset IdentifierUse v4.0.1 (ValueSet)

This value set contains 2 concepts

SystemCodeDisplay (en)DefinitionJSONXML
http://hl7.org/fhir/identifier-use  officialOfficialThe identifier considered to be most trusted for the identification of this item. Sometimes also known as "primary" and "main". The determination of "official" is subjective and implementation guides often provide additional guidelines for use.
http://hl7.org/fhir/identifier-use  secondarySecondaryAn identifier that was assigned in secondary use - it serves to identify the object in a relative context, but cannot be consistently assigned to the same object again in a different context.

Expansion performed internally based on codesystem BC Client Registry merge status. v1.0.0 (CodeSystem)

This value set contains 2 concepts

SystemCodeDisplay (en)DefinitionJSONXML
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-merge-status-code-system  obsoleteobsoleteThe merge obsoletes this Patient.
https://terminology.hlth.gov.bc.ca/ClientRegistry/CodeSystem/bc-client-registry-merge-status-code-system  cancelledcancelledThe merge cancels this Patient.

Expansion performed internally based on:

  • codesystem NameUse v4.0.1 (CodeSystem)
  • valueset NameUse v4.0.1 (ValueSet)

This value set contains 3 concepts

SystemCodeDisplay (en)DefinitionJSONXML
http://hl7.org/fhir/name-use  usualUsualKnown as/conventional/the one you normally use.
http://hl7.org/fhir/name-use  officialOfficialThe formal name as registered in an official (government) registry, but which name might not be commonly used. May be called "legal name".
http://hl7.org/fhir/name-use  nicknameNicknameA name that is used to address the person in an informal manner, but is not part of their formal or usual name.
